chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI S.M.A. No. 204 of 2019 Date Order with signature of Judge For hearing of main petition. 19.09.2019 Mr. Shaikh Muhammad Rafiq, advocate for petitioner. ----------- Through the instant petition, Petitioner Imran Ahmed has prayed for the grant of Letter of Administration in respect of immoveable property/asset, details whereof are mentioned in the Schedule of Property attached with the memo of petition, available at page 27, which was left by his deceased father, namely, Iqbal Hussain s/o. Nazeer Hussain, who allegedly died on 19.05.2018 at Karachi, leaving behind Fozia Tariq (daughter), Imran Ahmed (son/petitioner), Irfan Ahmed (son), Asma Iqbal (daughter), Uzma Faheem (daughter), Zubi Fahad (daughter) and Hina Ahmed (daughter) as his legal heirs. Original Death Certificate of deceased as well as photostat copies of Family Registration Certificate, issued by the NADRA and title documents of the immovable property are also filed at page 17, 23, 25 and 35 to 51. Legal heirs No. 1 and 3 to 7 have filed their affidavits of no objection for the grant of Letter of Administration in favour of the petitioner. Two witnesses, namely, Muhammad Tariq s/o. Muhammad Rafiq and Anees Ahmed Khan s/o. Ashiqullah Khan have also filed their respective affidavits and affirmed that there is no other legal 2 heir of the deceased except mentioned above. Their affidavits are also available on record. Notice of this petition was published in Daily “Jang” Karachi dated 23.04.2019; however, none has filed any objection to the grant of Letter of Administration to petitioner, as per the report submitted by the Deputy Registrar (OS) on 09.05.2019.

Their affidavits are also available on record. Notice of this petition was published in Daily “Jang” Karachi dated 23.04.2019; however, none has filed any objection to the grant of Letter of Administration to petitioner, as per the report submitted by the Deputy Registrar (OS) on 09.05.2019. Today petitioner and legal heir No. 1 and 3 to 7 as well as above named witnesses are present. They affirm the contents of memo of petition as well as their affidavits and also verify their signatures thereon. Since all the legal formalities/requirements have been fulfilled, there is no impediment in grant of Letter of Administration to petitioner, therefore, the petition is allowed. The office is directed to issue Letter of Administration in respect of immovable property/assets shown in the Schedule of Property, as per rules. J U D G E Athar Zai
